seoTitle: Point Cook vs Point Leo — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Point Cook and Point Leo. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Point Cook vs Point Leo: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Point Cook and Point Leo.
heading: Point Cook vs Point Leo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Point Cook, Victoria and Point Leo, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Point Leo has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Point Cook (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Point Cook or Point Leo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Point Cook has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Point Leo scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Point Cook scores 48/100 for bushfire risk. Point Leo scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Point Cook or Point Leo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Point Leo has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
